codeReview.summary: The fix improves coverage breadth, but it still does not capture emitted runtime logs from actual logging callsites/sinks on startup and sync paths. Both updated suites primarily push manually assembled strings into a test-local collector, so regressions in real log emission would still be missed.

issues.description: The new RuntimeLogCapture is not wired to any production logger/sink; it only appends manually provided strings. Tests then feed profile fields/state via helper calls (e.g., lines 263, 267, 293, 327), which does not assert what runtime startup/sync logging paths actually emit.

UI-level VAL-CROSS-006 checks also use a test-local RuntimeLogCapture populated by direct helper invocations (lines 1340-1345) rather than intercepting emitted runtime logs. This remains helper-driven and does not satisfy deterministic log-sink interception expected by the contract.

The test logs `filteredEnv` before redaction and then asserts secret-pattern absence across captured text, making outcomes environment-dependent (passes when secrets are absent, can fail when host env contains real secrets) and not representative of actual redacted logging behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `followedProcedure=true`, but handoff verification still omits context-read evidence required by the skill's handoff discipline.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/switcher-integration-worker/SKILL.md requires concrete context-read commands when `followedProcedure=true`; this handoff's `verification.commandsRun` only lists two xcodebuild test commands.

summary: Fix review failed. Compared with the original failed commit (791ea95...), this revision broadens tests but still does not capture real emitted runtime logs from startup/sync logging paths, so the prior VAL-CROSS-006 failure remains unresolved.
